report_name: FCC Reviews Telecom Supply Chain Security Reporting to Counter Espionage Threats

identities.name: Federal Communications Commission
identities.description: U.S. federal agency reviewing information collection requirements for its supply chain security programs to enhance oversight of telecommunications infrastructure.

identities.name: Federal Register
identities.description: The official journal of the U.S. federal government where the FCC published its notice for public comment.

identities.name: Huawei
identities.description: A manufacturer of telecommunications equipment deemed a threat to U.S. national security, whose products are targeted for removal and replacement under the FCC's reimbursement program.

identities.name: ZTE
identities.description: A manufacturer of telecommunications equipment deemed a threat to U.S. national security, whose products are targeted for removal and replacement under the FCC's reimbursement program.

identities.name: U.S. telecommunications service providers
identities.description: The class of organizations affected by the FCC's supply chain security reporting requirements, especially rural and smaller carriers participating in federal reimbursement programs.
